← Back to list
MemTensor/MemOS ·GitHub ⭐ 6797 Python · Memory, LLM, AI-Agent, RAG, Research, Python, Open-Source

What it is

MemOS is an open-source Memory Operating System (arXiv 2507.03724) for LLMs and AI agents, providing a unified API for multi-modal memory (text, images, tools, personas) with multi-cube knowledge base management, async ingestion, feedback/correction, and native OpenClaw plugin support.

Problems solved

  • Solves the fundamental problem of LLM statefulness: agents have no persistent
  • cross-session
  • structured memory. MemOS provides graph-structured
  • inspectable memory with 43.7% better accuracy than OpenAI Memory
  • 35.24% token savings
  • and a +2568% improvement on PrefEval-10.

How to get started

Cloud: Sign up on MemOS Dashboard, get API key, follow Cloud Getting Started guide. Self-hosted: git clone, configure .env, docker compose up. Use via REST API to add/search user memories, or install the OpenClaw plugin (Cloud or Local).

Detailed report

1. Positioning

MemTensor/MemOS is positioned as a memos is an open-source memory operating system (arxiv 2507.03724) for llms and ai agents, providing a unified api for multi-modal memory (text, images, tools, personas) with multi-cube knowledge base management, async ingestion, feedback/correction, and native openclaw plugin support. It aims to provide developers with a robust, Python-based solution for ai memory os for llm and agent systems(moltbot,clawdbot,openclaw), enabling persistent skill memory for cross-task skill reuse and evolution.

2. Pain Points & Advantages

Traditional methods in this domain often suffer from Solves the fundamental problem of LLM statefulness: agents have no persistent. MemTensor/MemOS addresses this by memos is an open-source memory operating system (arxiv 2507.03724) for llms and ai agents, providing a unified api for multi-modal memory (text, images, tools, personas) with multi-cube knowledge base management, async ingestion, feedback/correction, and native openclaw plugin support, offering a significant performance boost and better developer ergonomics compared to existing alternatives.

3. Technical Architecture

The project is built using Python, adopting a modular and scalable design. It leverages modern industry standards to ensure compatibility across different environments, focusing on efficiency and ease of integration.

4. Core Implementation

Main logic revolves around ai memory os for llm and agent systems(moltbot,clawdbot,openclaw), enabling persistent skill memory for cross-task skill reuse and evolution. The implementation utilizes Python's strengths to provide memos is an open-source memory operating system (arxiv 2507.03724) for llms and ai agents, providing a unified api for multi-modal memory (text, images, tools, personas) with multi-cube knowledge base management, async ingestion, feedback/correction, and native openclaw plugin support, ensuring high reliability and performance under load.

5. Code Organization

The repository is structured logically, with core functionality separated from utilities and examples. This organization facilitates easy navigation and contribution for developers looking to extend the tool's capabilities.

6. Quick Start Suggestion

To get started, cloud: sign up on memos dashboard, get api key, follow cloud getting started guide. self-hosted: git clone, configure .env, docker compose up. use via rest api to add/search user memories, or install the openclaw plugin (cloud or local). This will allow you to quickly evaluate the tool and integrate it into your existing development workflow.

7. Summary

An essential, Python-powered utility that streamlines ai memory os for llm and agent systems(moltbot,clawdbot,openclaw), enabling persistent skill memory for cross-task skill reuse and evolution; it effectively tackles Solves the fundamental problem of LLM statefulness: agents have no persistent while maintaining a high standard of code quality and usability.
